title: Rosa × dumetorum decalvata
intro: Rosa × dumetorum decalvata, also known as the wild rose or European rose, is a deciduous shrub species in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Rosa × dumetorum decalvata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include rose tea, rose petal infusions, and rosehip tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and support immune function.
uses: Rosa × dumetorum decalvata has been used traditionally in folk medicine, but its medicinal properties are not extensively documented. The plant's extracts have shown potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which could contribute to its therapeutic effects. Rosa × dumetorum decalvata is also reported to possess antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and infection prevention. Further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent. While some studies suggest its potential benefits, more extensive clinical trials are required to fully understand its medicinal uses.

constituents: Rosa × dumetorum decalvata, also known as a variant of the wild rose, is believed to contain a range of active constituents that contribute to its potential health benefits. These constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other plant-derived compounds that may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's active constituents may also include fragrances such as geraniol and beta-damascenone, which are responsible for its distinctive scent. Additionally, the plant may contain other bioactive compounds like salicylic acid and ellagic acid, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents of Rosa × dumetorum decalvata and their potential health benefits.
